skill.name: architecture-patterns
skill.description: Implement proven backend architecture patterns including Clean Architecture, Hexagonal Architecture, and Domain-Driven Design. Use when architecting complex backend systems or refactoring existing applications for better maintainability.
skill.summary: Design maintainable backend systems with Clean Architecture, Hexagonal Architecture, and Domain-Driven Design.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, environment access, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review shows these are documentation examples and terminology matches. No prompt injection, hardcoded secret, executable script, or malicious behavior was found. The remaining concern is low: one example assigns a supplied Stripe API key without secret-management guidance.

low_findings.title: API Key Handling Guidance Is Incomplete
low_findings.description: The Stripe adapter example accepts an api_key parameter and assigns it to the Stripe client. No hardcoded secret or environment read was found, but the documentation should remind users to load secrets from approved secret storage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code sample clearly handles an API key, but it is illustrative documentation and does not collect or exfiltrate secrets. The risk is limited to users copying the pattern without secret-management safeguards.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Markdown code fences or inline resource paths. They do not execute commands and contain architecture examples, not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every listed location is either a Markdown fence or an inline file reference. There is no executable Ruby, shell invocation, or command interpolation at those lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Architecture Terminology Reported as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits are plain English architecture terms such as Domain-Driven Design and Designing. No cryptographic API, hash algorithm, or encryption implementation was found at the reported lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain headings, description text, and usage bullets. They do not mention MD5, SHA-1, DES usage, or any cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Sample Application Code Reported as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance hits occur in Python sample code for UUID creation, SQL queries, validation errors, and object identifiers. No system enumeration, host inspection, or environment probing was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contexts are business-domain examples and parameterized SQL snippets. They do not inspect the local system or enumerate files, processes, users, or networks.

content.value_statement: Complex backend systems often become hard to test, change, and explain. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through proven architecture patterns and practical migration steps.
actual_capabilities: Explains Clean Architecture layers and dependency rules.
Maps Hexagonal Architecture ports, adapters, and domain boundaries.
Describes Domain-Driven Design entities, value objects, aggregates, and events.
Provides backend directory structures and interface examples.
Outlines migration and validation steps for refactoring backend systems.
Lists best practices and common pitfalls for architecture adoption.
limitations: It focuses on backend architecture, not frontend application structure.
It provides guidance and examples, not a complete generated application.
It does not replace domain discovery with product and engineering stakeholders.
Example integrations need security, observability, and deployment hardening before production use.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Backend
use_cases.description: Choose a structure for services, domain logic, repositories, and adapters before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Refactor a Monolith
use_cases.description: Separate business rules from controllers, databases, and frameworks without rewriting the full system at once.

use_cases.title: Standardize Architecture Reviews
use_cases.description: Create consistent review criteria for dependency direction, module boundaries, and domain model quality.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Pattern
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner evaluation for a new backend service.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the architecture-patterns skill to compare Clean Architecture, Hexagonal Architecture, and Domain-Driven Design for this backend: [describe domain, team size, integrations, and expected scale]. Recommend one approach and explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Define Boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the architecture-patterns skill to identify bounded contexts, core entities, value objects, repositories, and external adapters for this domain: [describe workflows and business rules].

prompt_templates.title: Create a Refactoring Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the architecture-patterns skill to create a phased refactoring plan for this backend structure: [paste file tree and pain points]. Include dependency rules, risk controls, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced governance for an existing system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the architecture-patterns skill to review this backend architecture: [describe modules, dependencies, database access, and integration points]. Identify boundary violations, over-engineering, missing abstractions, and concrete fixes.

output_examples.input: Review a payment service that mixes controller logic, database calls, and payment provider calls.
output_examples.output: The review separates domain rules from infrastructure, proposes ports for payments and persistence, and lists a phased migration plan.

output_examples.input: Design a new order management backend with customer, payment, and notification workflows.
output_examples.output: The output defines bounded contexts, aggregate roots, repository contracts, adapters, and dependency rules for a maintainable implementation.

output_examples.input: Create team guidelines for applying Clean Architecture in Python services.
output_examples.output: The guidance covers layer responsibilities, folder structure, testing boundaries, adapter rules, and common mistakes to avoid.

best_practices: Start with domain boundaries and business rules before choosing frameworks.
Keep dependencies pointing toward the domain core.
Use small interfaces and tests to validate each architectural boundary.
anti_patterns: Do not apply full Clean Architecture to simple CRUD services without clear benefits.
Do not let controllers, ORMs, or external APIs define domain behavior.
Do not create generic abstractions that hide important business language.

faq.question: What architecture patterns does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Clean Architecture, Hexagonal Architecture, and Domain-Driven Design for backend systems.

faq.question: Can it generate production-ready code?
faq.answer: It provides examples and design guidance, but production code needs project-specific security, testing, and deployment work.

faq.question: Is this useful for small applications?
faq.answer: It can help with boundaries, but full architecture patterns may be too heavy for small CRUD applications.

faq.question: Does it support refactoring existing systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create phased migration plans that reduce coupling while preserving current behavior.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it include security architecture guidance?
faq.answer: Only indirectly. Security controls, secret management, and threat modeling should be added during implementation.
